_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

claim.label: Post procedure clinical photograph showing evidence of surgery

ppd_preauth.q: Was the patient's CT/MRI report indicative of the procedure?

common_queries: Patient's CT/MRI report not indicative of the procedure
Relevant Investigations (CT/MRI) not submitted at pre-authorization
Post-procedure imaging (CT/MRI) not submitted at claim stage
Post-procedure clinical photograph showing evidence of surgery not submitted at claim stage
Detailed procedure/operative notes not submitted at claim stage

clinical_key_pointers: Chronic subdural hematoma (cSDH) is a chronic (≥3 weeks) intracranial bleeding between the dura mater (which adheres to the skull), and the arachnoid mater (which envelops the brain). The underlying cause of cSDH is usually traumatic tearing of the bridging veins which connect the brain surface with the dura mater.
Common symptoms include headache, lethargy, memory impairment, dizziness, confusion, weakness, nausea, vomiting, impaired vision, and seizures.
Patients with large hematomas may develop varying degrees of paralysis and coma.
cSDH may mimic other brain diseases like dementia, stroke, TIA, encephalitis, tumors, or abscesses.
Craniotomy is a neurosurgical procedure to temporarily remove a section of the skull to access and remove the hematoma.
